 Electronic Arts [...] ## Highest rated rpgs

Highest rated

Among top-reviewed RPG picks, Talkie: Soulful AI, StartPlaying.Games, and itch.io span solo, group, and creator needs. Talkie centers on customizable AI companions for immersive, emotionally responsive role-play. StartPlaying streamlines finding pro Game Masters and systems for scheduled campaigns. itch.io empowers indie RPG discovery and distribution with community tools and flexible hosting for experimental experiences.

Samantha Thomas
Oleg Pavlov
Graphite

Graphite [...] Need more play time for this :)

Carlos Tamaoki

This is almost like like FTL for people who are more into businesses than spaceships. If running a virtual company is your idea of having fun, this is a one-of-a-king game to try.
With a little more polish in the UX side and some tweaks with the help of an economics professor and they could easily have this game be used as a simulator on starting-level business management courses.

Lots of things to do and relatively realistic. Gives a look at what is life in a webservices company.

The interface and overall UX is bit peculiar.

results.title: Pitch Lucy AI: Adversarial Agent Shark Tank Game | Product Hunt
results.content:
The combination of gaming mechanics with actual investment concepts could be a great way for people to practice their pitching skills and learn about token fundamentals in a low-risk environment. Really smart way to gamify learning in the Web3 space.

@Mariano - curious about Lucy's investment criteria and how the AI evaluates pitches. Any plans to add multiplayer features or leaderboards to increase competition? Would be fascinating to see which pitch strategies work best against the AI! 🎮🚀"

The intersection of Investing, Games, and Web3 tags suggests they're targeting a growing niche of users interested in both crypto and gamified learning experiences. Clever use of AI to create an engaging educational tool for the Web3 space.

Edgar Abasov [...] Pitch Lucy AI

been using it for years. it's free, it's efficient, it works well, it's easy to fix mistakes, and the developers really seem to care about making it a good product rather than making money off of it. no ads, no hidden feens, literally just a good product that's easy to use. to the devs, thank you so much. you have made my life so much easier with school and creative projects. y'all deserve all the best.

The idea is super cool, however, because it's too simple (much more like a todo list than a project management tool) it doesn't help a project manager to keep track of needed information!

Pain points:

- Cannot assign tasks to someone;

- Cannot comment on tasks;

- Cannot tag tasks or projects;

- Cannot move tasks back to the todo list; [...] Report

Dinuka Jay

VibeAtWork

VibeAtWork

Maker

Thanks for the feedback. Of course, these will be added eventually :) We prioritized the features we've shipped as a MVP for indie makers to get started. Day after day we are releasing each one of those additional features. In fact, today we are shipping out the 4th point you mentioned: Moving tasks back to todo list. Appreciate the feedback and pointing these out. Will proceed to shipping these one by one :)
